tasks_completed.description: Fix test_fails_without_session_id to strip both CLAUDE_SESSION_ID and CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID
tasks_completed.changes: Line 189: changed `if k != "CLAUDE_SESSION_ID"` to `if k not in ("CLAUDE_SESSION_ID", "CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID")`
tasks_completed.rationale: Root cause from commit 21500b1: CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID was added as a fallback to _resolve_sid() in write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py without updating the test. The test env strip must mirror the full set of env vars the function checks.

dev.implementation_summary: Single-line test fix: the env dict comprehension in test_fails_without_session_id now filters out both CLAUDE_SESSION_ID and CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID. This allows the test to correctly reproduce the 'neither SID is available' failure path when running in Claude Code agent contexts where CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID is always present.

ac_verification.AC1: pass — pytest hooks/tests/test_bulk_commit_sentinel.py::TestWriteBulkCommitSentinelScript::test_fails_without_session_id shows PASSED (1 passed in 0.03s)
ac_verification.AC2: pass — pytest hooks/tests/test_bulk_commit_sentinel.py shows 15 passed, 0 failed (0.04s)

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: 21500b1 - fix(hooks,agents,tests): extend write-bulk-commit-sentinel guard with venv allowlist and agent DO NOT items
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID was added as a fallback env var to _resolve_sid() in write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py to support environments where only CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID is available. The test was not updated to strip both env vars, so in Claude Code agent contexts where CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID is always set, the writer resolves successfully and exits 0 instead of the expected 2.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: The test env construction now strips both env vars from os.environ, ensuring the writer reaches its 'cannot resolve SID' failure path regardless of which session ID env vars are present in the host environment.

self_verification.notes: AC1: 1 passed in 0.03s. AC2: 15 passed, 0 failed in 0.04s.

dev.qa_notes: Run both pytest commands in acceptance criteria. No behavioral logic changed — test-only fix.

recommendations: Consider adding a positive regression test for CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID fallback (codex finding 2 — out of scope for this cycle but valid future work)
Consider adding a test for empty-string env vars (codex finding 3 — out of scope for this cycle)

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex raised 3 findings: (1) test could be made more hermetic by using clear=True with empty dict; (2) no positive regression test for CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID fallback; (3) empty env var case not covered. All findings have PROPOSED_FIX values.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 3 findings classified as out_of_scope per BA spec constraint: 'No changes to test logic other than the env-strip set in test_fails_without_session_id.' Finding 1 (hermetic) is also a style preference — the existing clear=True+filter approach is already correct. Findings 2 and 3 (new tests) are explicit scope exclusions in the BA spec. All findings surfaced to recommendations for future cycles.

codex_out_of_scope.finding: Make test hermetic with patch.dict(os.environ, {}, clear=True) instead of filter approach
codex_out_of_scope.reason: Style preference; BA spec constrains change to the filter expression only. Current approach is functionally correct.

codex_out_of_scope.finding: Add positive regression test for CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID fallback (sets only CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID, asserts sentinel sid matches)
codex_out_of_scope.reason: BA spec explicitly excludes 'any changes to test logic other than the env-strip set in test_fails_without_session_id'. Adding new test methods is scope expansion.

codex_out_of_scope.finding: Add test_fails_with_empty_session_ids for empty-string env var case
codex_out_of_scope.reason: Same BA spec exclusion — adding new test methods is out of scope for this cycle.
